Arterial supply to the anal canal includes contributions from the superior, middle, and inferior rectal arteries, though none of the listed options explicitly reference arterial branches beyond general categorization. However, since the evidence does not support any one artery being uniquely emphasized over others nor confirm direct arterial supply details, and given that hemorrhoid-related procedures target venous pathways rather than arterial ones, we must infer that while multiple vessels contribute to anal circulation, only the venous aspects are clinically highlighted here.
